clinical_coherence.notes: 71M Hispanic with HTN, DM2, OSA, mild AS, and non-obstructive CAD. ECG shows bifascicular block (RBBB + LAFB) with first-degree AV block (trifascicular pattern). Recurrent syncope with brief convulsive movements. Prior misdiagnosis as orthostatic hypotension by PCP adds realistic diagnostic journey. Holter captures intermittent Mobitz type II and transient complete heart block.

tool_output_consistency.notes: ECG shows trifascicular pattern. Holter captures intermittent Mobitz type II and one episode of transient CHB. EP study confirms prolonged HV interval. EEG normal. Labs show hypokalemia (3.3) and hypomagnesemia (1.7) from thiazide. All outputs concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: R55 ICD code acceptable. Diagnosis of intermittent high-degree AV block in trifascicular disease is correct. Critical actions include electrolyte correction, holding thiazide, and pacemaker implantation.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Moderate difficulty appropriate. Intermittent conduction disease requires Holter for capture. Prior PCP misdiagnosis as orthostatic hypotension adds clinical realism and demonstrates common diagnostic pitfalls.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 71M Hispanic with cardiovascular comorbidities and multiple medications is realistic. Adds ethnic diversity.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case for trifascicular disease with intermittent high-degree AV block. Similar to SYNC-CARD-M01 in structure, limiting novelty. The prior misdiagnosis as orthostatic hypotension is a good teaching point about common diagnostic errors.

issues_found: Minor: Clinical pattern closely resembles SYNC-CARD-M01, reducing case set diversity at the moderate tier
strengths: Realistic prior misdiagnosis as orthostatic hypotension
Transient complete heart block captured on Holter adds prognostic urgency
Electrolyte management component with thiazide-induced depletion
